title: DCSync Rights Granted to Unprivileged Account via dacledit
description: Detects modification of domain object DACL to grant DS-Replication permissions to a non-privileged account. Enables DCSync attacks from any machine with that account's credentials.
remediation: Audit and alert on Event ID 5136 for domain object ACL changes
Restrict WriteDACL permission on domain object to DA only
Monitor dacledit tool execution via process creation events

title: Unauthorized Group Membership Addition — GenericAll/GenericWrite Abuse
description: Detects adding unauthorized users to privileged groups using GenericAll or GenericWrite ACE over the target group. Enables privilege escalation by adding attacker account to Domain Admins, Enterprise Admins, etc.
remediation: Alert on Event IDs 4728/4732/4756 for sensitive groups immediately
Implement Privileged Identity Management (PIM) for group membership changes
Audit GenericAll/GenericWrite ACEs on privileged groups

title: Force Password Change — ExtendedRight Abuse
description: Detects forced password changes using ExtendedRight "User-Force-Change-Password" ACE. Attacker changes target account password without knowing current password, enabling account takeover.
remediation: Audit User-Force-Change-Password ACE on all user objects
Alert on Event ID 4724 where subject != target
Implement time-based alerting for off-hours password changes

title: Shadow Credentials — msDS-KeyCredentialLink Attribute Injection
description: Detects injection of attacker-controlled key into msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute (Windows Hello for Business). Enables Kerberos pre-auth using certificate without knowing account password. Works via certipy shadow auto.
remediation: Alert on any msDS-KeyCredentialLink modification (Event 5136)
Restrict write access to msDS-KeyCredentialLink attribute
Enable audit logging for AD attribute changes (Advanced Audit Policy)

title: Computer Account Creation via MachineAccountQuota
description: Detects creation of computer accounts by non-privileged users exploiting the default MachineAccountQuota=10. Used as prerequisite for RBCD attacks and other computer-account-dependent escalations.
remediation: Set MachineAccountQuota to 0 (prevents non-admin computer account creation)
Set-ADDomain -Identity {domain} -Replace @{'ms-DS-MachineAccountQuota'=0}
Monitor Event ID 4741 for unexpected computer account creation

title: Unauthorized LAPS Password Read via LDAP
description: Detects unauthorized reading of ms-Mcs-AdmPwd (LAPS password) attribute from Active Directory. Provides local admin credentials for LAPS-managed computers.
remediation: Enable LAPS attribute access auditing
Restrict ms-Mcs-AdmPwd read permissions to authorized groups only
Rotate LAPS passwords immediately after suspected compromise

title: BloodHound LDAP Collection — ACL Enumeration for Attack Path Discovery
description: Detects BloodHound data collection which queries all AD objects, ACLs, group memberships, and computer sessions via LDAP. Creates large number of LDAP queries in short time.
remediation: Monitor for bulk LDAP queries from non-DC hosts
Implement Microsoft Defender for Identity BloodHound detection
Deploy AD Canary objects that alert on enumeration

title: ESC1 — Certificate Enrollment with Arbitrary SAN (Domain Admin Impersonation)
description: ESC1: Certificate template allows requestor to specify Subject Alternative Name (SAN) with CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT. Attacker enrolls certificate as Domain Admin by specifying admin UPN in SAN field.
remediation: Remove CT_FLAG_ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT from template flags
Require CA Manager Approval for sensitive templates
Enable "Auditing" on all certificate templates

title: ESC4 — Certificate Template ACL Overwrite (WriteDACL/WriteOwner/GenericWrite)
description: ESC4: Attacker has WriteDACL, WriteOwner, or GenericWrite over a certificate template. Modifies template to grant ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT, then requests certificate as DA. Certipy template command with -save-old preserves original.
remediation: Audit and alert on Event 5136 for pKICertificateTemplate object modifications
Restrict template modification to PKI Admins and Enterprise Admins only
Review template ACLs regularly with Certify or certipy find

title: ESC6 — CA Flag Abuse (EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2)
description: ESC6: CA has EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 flag set, allowing SAN in any request even if template doesn't have ENROLLEE_SUPPLIES_SUBJECT. Equivalent impact to ESC1 but at CA level.
remediation: Remove EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 flag from CA
certutil -setreg policy\\EditFlags -EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2
Restart CertSvc after change

title: ESC8 — NTLM Relay to ADCS Web Enrollment for DC Certificate
description: ESC8: ADCS web enrollment endpoint (certsrv) accepts NTLM authentication. Attacker relays DC NTLM authentication (via PrinterBug/PetitPotam) to ADCS, obtains DC certificate, then uses PKINIT to get DC TGT → DCSync.
remediation: Enable HTTPS on ADCS web enrollment (disable HTTP)
Enable EPA (Extended Protection for Authentication) on IIS
Require Kerberos authentication for ADCS web enrollment

title: ESC9 — No Security Extension (SID Binding Bypass)
description: ESC9: Template has CT_FLAG_NO_SECURITY_EXTENSION (0x80000) in msPKI-Enrollment-Flag. Issued cert lacks szOID_NTDS_CA_SECURITY_EXT SID binding, allowing cert to be used for auth as different account if GenericWrite exists on target.
remediation: Require CA to mark certificates with szOID_NTDS_CA_SECURITY_EXT
Apply May 2022 cumulative update (KB5014754) — StrongCertificateBindingEnforcement

title: ESC11 — RPC Relay to Certificate Enrollment (IF_ENFORCEENCRYPTICERTREQUEST)
description: ESC11: CA does not have IF_ENFORCEENCRYPTICERTREQUEST flag, enabling NTLM relay to RPC-based certificate enrollment (MS-ICPR) without encryption. Attacker relays SMB/HTTP auth to obtain arbitrary certificate.
remediation: Set IF_ENFORCEENCRYPTICERTREQUEST flag on CA
Enable DCOM authentication level >= Packet Privacy

title: PKINIT Authentication — Certificate Used for Kerberos TGT
description: Detects use of certificate for Kerberos pre-authentication (PKINIT). Used post-ESC1/ESC6/Shadow Credentials to get TGT. UnPAC-the-Hash extracts NTLM hash from the TGT without touching LSASS.
remediation: Monitor Event 4768 with certificate fields populated
Alert on PKINIT authentication for accounts not enrolled in Smart Card auth

title: ADCS Template Enumeration — Certipy find
description: Detects ADCS template enumeration via certipy find or Certify.exe. Generates LDAP queries against PKI container for template attributes.
remediation: Monitor bulk pKICertificateTemplate LDAP reads
Implement AD CS Canary templates that alert on enumeration
sast_code_analysis:

title: MS-RPRN PrinterBug — Force DC Authentication via Print Spooler
description: MS-RPRN's RpcRemoteFindFirstPrinterChangeNotification API forces target to authenticate to attacker-controlled host. Used to capture NTLMv2 hash or relay to LDAP/ADCS. Requires Print Spooler running on target DC.
commands: crackmapexec smb {dc} -u {user} -p {pass} -M spooler  # Check if vulnerable
all_commands: crackmapexec smb {dc} -u {user} -p {pass} -M spooler  # Check if vulnerable
remediation: Disable Print Spooler service on all Domain Controllers (not needed)
Block SMB outbound from DCs to non-DC hosts (firewall rule)
Apply PrintNightmare patches and disable outbound SMB from DCs

title: PetitPotam — EfsRpc Forced Authentication (Authenticated and Unauthenticated)
description: PetitPotam exploits MS-EFSRPC (EfsRpcOpenFileRaw) to force target to authenticate to attacker. Can work unauthenticated on older Windows versions. Used for NTLM relay to LDAP or ADCS to compromise domain.
remediation: Apply MS KB5005413 (PetitPotam patches)
Block LSASS outbound SMB connections via firewall
Disable EFS if not required

title: DFSCoerce — NetrDfsAddStdRoot MS-DFSNM Forced Authentication
description: DFSCoerce exploits MS-DFSNM NetrDfsAddStdRoot/NetrDfsRemoveStdRoot to force authentication. Authenticated vector. Works even when PetitPotam is patched.
remediation: Disable DFS-N service on DCs if not required
Block outbound SMB from DCs via Windows Firewall
Monitor DFS RPC interface calls from external hosts

title: Coercer Framework — Automated Multi-Protocol Coercion Scan and Exploit
description: Coercer automatically tries all known coercion methods (MS-RPRN, MS-EFSRPC, MS-DFSNM, MS-FSRVP, MS-TSCH, etc.) against target. -scan mode passive detection, no authentication attempt. High-value target: DCs.
remediation: Block all coercible RPC interfaces at network perimeter
Disable unnecessary services (Spooler, EFS, DFS-N) on DCs

title: Responder — LLMNR/NBT-NS Poisoning for NTLMv2 Hash Capture
description: Responder poisons LLMNR and NBT-NS name resolution to redirect victims to attacker's SMB/HTTP server for NTLMv2 hash capture. Combined with coercion for targeted hash collection.
all_commands: Combined with coercion to capture DC$ NTLMv2 hash
remediation: Disable LLMNR via Group Policy: Computer Configuration → Administrative Templates → Network → DNS Client → Turn off multicast name resolution
Disable NetBIOS over TCP/IP (disable NBT-NS)
Monitor for unexpected LLMNR/NBT-NS responses on network

title: NTLM Relay to LDAP — Computer Account / DCSync Rights
description: NTLM relay to LDAP/LDAPS allows attacker to authenticate as victim (typically DC$) and perform LDAP operations: add computer accounts, grant DCSync rights, set RBCD, or modify group membership. impacket-ntlmrelayx --delegate-access enables RBCD relay.
remediation: Enable LDAP signing (require signing for LDAP clients)
Enable LDAP channel binding (CVE-2017-8563 mitigations)
Enforce SMB signing (prevents relay from SMB sources)

title: NTLM Relay to ADCS — DC Certificate Enrollment for DA Takeover
description: Most impactful relay attack: coerce DC$ authentication, relay to ADCS web enrollment, obtain DC certificate, use PKINIT to get DC TGT, then DCSync all hashes. Full domain compromise in minutes. Attack chain: PetitPotam → ntlmrelayx --adcs → certipy auth → secretsdump
commands: certipy auth -pfx dc.pfx -dc-ip {dc} -domain {dom}  # Get DC TGT
all_commands: certipy auth -pfx dc.pfx -dc-ip {dc} -domain {dom}  # Get DC TGT
remediation: Enable HTTPS + EPA on ADCS Web Enrollment (prevents relay)
Require Kerberos for ADCS enrollment
Monitor ADCS Event 4887 for cross-machine DC cert issuance

title: NTLM Relay with Shadow Credentials — msDS-KeyCredentialLink via Relay
description: ntlmrelayx --shadow-credentials injects attacker certificate into msDS-KeyCredentialLink of target account (usually DC$) during relay. Enables PKINIT authentication as DC$ → DCSync.
remediation: Enable LDAP signing + channel binding
Monitor msDS-KeyCredentialLink modifications (Event 5136)
Alert on computer accounts modifying other computer accounts' attributes

title: Unconstrained Delegation TGT Capture via Coercion
description: Rubeus monitor on unconstrained delegation host captures forwarded TGT when coercion forces DC to authenticate. Captured TGT used for Pass-the-Ticket → DCSync or domain compromise.
remediation: Remove Unconstrained Delegation from non-DC computers
Enable Protected Users group for privileged service accounts
Monitor Rubeus.exe execution

title: LSASS Memory Access — Credential Extraction
description: Detects attempts to read LSASS process memory for credential extraction. Covers Mimikatz, lsassy, nanodump, PPLdump, and SafetyKatz techniques.
remediation: Enable RunAsPPL (Protected Process Light) for LSASS
Deploy Credential Guard (virtualizes LSASS — no NTLM hashes in memory)
Block procdump.exe and nanodump.exe via AppLocker/WDAC

title: Invoke-Mimi PowerShell In-Memory Credential Dump
description: Detects Invoke-Mimi (PowerShell in-memory Mimikatz) execution for credential dumping. No binary written to disk — loaded via DownloadString.
remediation: Enable PowerShell Script Block Logging (GPO)
Enable PowerShell Module Logging
Use Constrained Language Mode (CLM) for PowerShell

title: SAM Registry Hive Access — Local Account Hash Extraction
description: Detects access to SAM registry hive for local account credential extraction. impacket-secretsdump uses remote registry service; reg save copies hive.
remediation: Restrict remote registry access via Group Policy
Monitor RemoteRegistry service start events
Alert on SAM/SECURITY hive access by non-SYSTEM accounts

title: DCSync — Replication-Based NTDS Hash Extraction
description: DCSync mimics DC replication to extract password hashes without touching NTDS.dit file. Requires DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All privilege. Used by impacket-secretsdump and mimikatz lsadump::dcsync.
remediation: Audit DS-Replication-Get-Changes ACE on domain object
Alert on 4662 with replication GUIDs from non-DC sources
Deploy Microsoft Defender for Identity DCSync detection

title: LSA Secrets Extraction via Remote Registry
description: LSA Secrets store service account credentials, DPAPI master keys, and cached domain credentials. Accessible via SECURITY hive or remote secretsdump with SYSTEM privileges.
remediation: Enable LSA Protection (PPL for LSA process)
Restrict remote registry access
Monitor access to SECURITY registry hive

title: LAPS Password — ms-Mcs-AdmPwd Attribute Read
description: Detects unauthorized reading of LAPS (Local Administrator Password Solution) attribute ms-Mcs-AdmPwd from Active Directory. Reading this attribute provides local admin credentials for managed computers.
remediation: Enable LAPS audit logging
Restrict ms-Mcs-AdmPwd read permissions to authorized accounts only
Migrate to Windows LAPS (newer, more secure)

title: AES Encryption Key Extraction — sekurlsa::ekeys
description: Extraction of AES128/AES256 Kerberos encryption keys from LSASS. Keys enable Overpass-the-Hash (Pass-the-Key), Silver Ticket, and S4U2Self attacks without needing plaintext passwords.
remediation: Enable Credential Guard (makes AES keys unavailable in user-mode LSASS)
Enable LSASS PPL protection (RunAsPPL)
Audit Loader.exe / SafetyKatz patterns

title: Windows Credential Vault Dump — vault::cred /patch
description: Dumps credentials stored in Windows Credential Manager via Mimikatz token::elevate + vault::cred /patch. Can extract credentials stored by scheduled tasks, services, and users via cmdkey.
remediation: Audit stored credentials (cmdkey /list)
Credential Guard protects domain credentials in vault
Restrict scheduled task credential storage

title: DCSync — Full Domain Hash Dump via Replication Protocol
description: DCSync uses MS-DRSR (Directory Replication Service Remote Protocol) to request password data as if replicating from a DC. Requires DS-Replication-Get-Changes-All privilege. Extracts NTLM hashes for all domain accounts including krbtgt.
remediation: Audit DS-Replication-Get-Changes ACE on domain object
Deploy Microsoft Defender for Identity DCSync alert
Monitor Event 4662 with replication GUIDs from non-DCs (immediate alert)

title: DCSync — Targeted Account Replication (krbtgt / Administrator)
description: Targeted DCSync requesting specific accounts (krbtgt, Administrator). More stealthy than full dump — fewer 4662 events generated.
remediation: Same as DCSync-all-001
Alert specifically when krbtgt or DA accounts are targeted via replication

title: DCShadow — Rogue Domain Controller Registration for Stealth AD Modification
description: DCShadow temporarily registers attacker machine as a Domain Controller using Mimikatz. Allows pushing arbitrary AD changes (group membership, SID history, password hash) that appear as legitimate DC replication — bypasses most SIEM auditing. Requires two simultaneous Mimikatz instances (SYSTEM + DA)
remediation: Monitor CN=Configuration for new nTDSDSA objects (rogue DC registration)
Alert on DCShadow via Microsoft Defender for Identity
Monitor for unexpected replication sources in Event 4929

title: DCSync Rights Enumeration — dacledit Read on Domain Object
description: Attacker enumerates which accounts have DS-Replication rights on domain object to identify existing DCSync-capable accounts or verify own rights.
remediation: Enumerate current replication rights regularly
Remove unauthorized DS-Replication ACEs immediately
sast_code_analysis:

title: Windows Defender Disabled via Set-MpPreference
description: Set-MpPreference used to disable Windows Defender real-time protection, IOAV protection, behavior monitoring, and cloud protection. Standard CRTP flow — first step before loading offensive tools.
remediation: Alert on Windows Defender Event 5001 (protection disabled) — critical
Monitor Set-MpPreference executions via PowerShell logging
Implement tamper protection (prevents modification via PS/API)

title: SafetyKatz via Loader.exe — In-Memory Credential Dump (No Disk Write)
description: SafetyKatz is modified Mimikatz that bypasses AV signatures. Loader.exe fetches it from HTTP (127.0.0.1:8080 via port forwarding) and loads directly into memory. No file written to disk — bypasses file-based AV.
remediation: Monitor Loader.exe execution and HTTP connections from process
Alert on .exe downloads from localhost (127.0.0.1) — unusual pattern
Deploy behavioral EDR that detects in-memory .NET assembly loading

title: AMSI Bypass — AmsiScanBuffer Patch in PowerShell Session
description: AMSI (Antimalware Scan Interface) bypass patches AmsiScanBuffer function in amsi.dll to return AMSI_RESULT_CLEAN for all scans. Multiple variants: reflection, direct patching, COM object manipulation.
remediation: Enable AMSI for all PowerShell versions (v5+)
Deploy EDR with AMSI integration
Enable Script Block Logging to detect AMSI bypass attempts

title: ETW Patching — Event Tracing for Windows Telemetry Disabled
description: ETW (Event Tracing for Windows) patching blinds EDR products that rely on ETW for telemetry. Patches EtwEventWrite in ntdll.dll to return immediately without writing events. Effective against CrowdStrike, Defender.
remediation: Deploy EDR with kernel-mode telemetry (not reliant on ETW)
Monitor ntdll.dll in-process modifications
Use Kernel Patch Protection (KPP / PatchGuard) on DCs

title: NTDLL Unhooking — Bypass EDR Userland Hooks
description: EDR products hook ntdll.dll syscall stubs to inspect API calls. NTDLL unhooking loads clean copy from disk and replaces hooked section. Hell's Gate uses direct syscalls to bypass hooks entirely.
remediation: Deploy kernel-mode EDR (EarlyBird injection detection)
Use ETW-based telemetry (harder to bypass than userland hooks)
Monitor for ntdll.dll loaded from non-standard paths

title: nanodump — Stealth LSASS Dump Bypassing Defender/CrowdStrike
description: nanodump uses invalid file signatures and process ID spoofing to dump LSASS while bypassing EDR. Creates a minidump without the MDMP signature that can be post-processed offline.
all_commands: nanodump.exe --valid"  # Valid minidump with fake signature
remediation: Enable RunAsPPL for LSASS
Deploy Credential Guard
Monitor LSASS access with non-standard GrantedAccess masks

title: Windows Event Log Clearing — Anti-Forensics
description: Clearing Windows event logs removes evidence of attacker activity. wevtutil.exe cl is the most common method. System event 1102 generated upon Security log clearing (requires auditing enabled).
remediation: Forward event logs to central SIEM immediately (before clearing)
Enable Event Log forwarding (WEF - Windows Event Forwarding)
Alert on Event 1102 immediately — critical indicator

title: RunAsPPL Disabled — LSASS PPL Protection Removed
description: RunAsPPL (Protected Process Light) prevents LSASS memory access. PPLKiller or registry modification disables this protection. After disable, full LSASS dump is possible.
remediation: Enable PPL Tamper Protection (requires Secure Boot)
Monitor RunAsPPL registry value (Event 4657)
Alert on PPLKiller.exe execution

title: Unauthorized GPO Creation and OU Linking
description: Attacker with GPO creation rights creates malicious GPO and links it to target OU. All computers/users in that OU execute GPO settings at next Group Policy refresh (90 minutes or forced via gpupdate).
remediation: Alert on Event 5137 for new GPO creation by non-admin accounts
Alert on Event 5136 for gPLink modifications on sensitive OUs
Restrict GPO creation rights in Group Policy Management

title: GPO Registry RunKey — Code Execution on Startup via Group Policy
description: Attacker abuses GPO to push registry RunKey to all computers in target OU. Executes payload on every system restart or group policy refresh. HKLM\\Software\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run via GPO is invisible to standard registry monitoring of directly-written keys.
remediation: Monitor SYSVOL for new Registry.xml files in Policy folders
Alert on RunKey additions via Group Policy
Review GPO Registry Preference settings regularly

title: GPO Immediate Scheduled Task — SharpGPOAbuse
description: SharpGPOAbuse creates an "Immediate" scheduled task via GPO that executes on next Group Policy refresh without waiting for reboot. Most immediate GPO code execution technique.
remediation: Monitor SYSVOL ScheduledTasks directories for new XML files
Alert on Event 4698 with non-standard task authors
Require change control for GPO scheduled task modifications

title: GPO Restricted Groups — Unauthorized Local Admin Addition
description: GPO Restricted Groups policy forces group membership on managed computers. Adding attacker account to Local Administrators via GPO grants local admin on all computers in linked OUs automatically.
remediation: Monitor GptTmpl.inf files in SYSVOL for Group Membership section changes
Alert on Event 4732 (Administrators) group additions from Group Policy

title: GPP CPassword — Encrypted Credentials in SYSVOL Group Policy Files
description: Group Policy Preferences (GPP) stored credentials in SYSVOL encrypted with static AES256 key published by Microsoft (MS14-025). Credentials from Groups.xml, Scheduledtasks.xml, Services.xml, DataSources.xml, Drives.xml can be decrypted by any domain user.
remediation: Apply MS14-025 patch (removes ability to create new GPP passwords)
Search and remove existing cpassword fields from SYSVOL
findstr /s /i cpassword \\\\{dom}\\sysvol\\{dom}\\policies\\ | select-string cpassword

title: GPO Delegation — Enumerate Write Permissions on Group Policy Objects
description: Bloodhound and PowerView enumerate GPO write permissions to find accounts with GPO modification rights. Any account with write access to a GPO linked to privileged OUs can compromise all computers in that OU.
remediation: Audit GPO write permissions via Group Policy Management Console
Remove non-standard accounts with GPO modification rights
Alert on bulk GPO ACL enumeration

title: AS-REP Roasting — Kerberos Pre-Auth Disabled Request
description: Detects AS-REP requests for accounts with Kerberos pre-authentication disabled. Attacker enumerates user list and collects crackable hashes offline via GetNPUsers (impacket) or Rubeus.
remediation: Enable Kerberos pre-authentication for ALL accounts
Audit accounts with DONT_REQ_PREAUTH flag via PowerShell
Get-ADUser -Filter {DoesNotRequirePreAuth -eq $true}

title: Kerberoasting — TGS Request for Service Accounts with SPN
description: Detects bulk TGS requests for service accounts with SPNs. Attacker uses authenticated account to request service tickets and cracks them offline. High number of TGS requests from single host is anomalous.
remediation: Use gMSA (Group Managed Service Accounts) — auto-rotating 240-char passwords
Set service account passwords to 30+ random characters
Monitor for RC4 TGS requests (prefer AES256)

title: Pass-the-Ticket — Kerberos Ticket Injection via klist/Rubeus
description: Detects Kerberos ticket injection into a logon session from outside the normal Kerberos authentication flow. Golden/Silver tickets and stolen .ccache files are injected to gain unauthorized access.
remediation: Enable Protected Users security group for privileged accounts
Implement Credential Guard (blocks ticket extraction from LSASS)
Monitor for anomalous Kerberos ticket lifetimes

title: Golden Ticket — Forged TGT Using krbtgt Hash
description: Detects creation and use of Golden Tickets — forged TGTs signed with the krbtgt account NTLM hash. Golden tickets can impersonate any user, survive password resets, and grant arbitrary group memberships.
remediation: Reset krbtgt password TWICE (invalidates all existing tickets)
Enable PAC validation (KB5008380)
Monitor for 4769 events without corresponding 4768

title: Silver Ticket — Forged Service Ticket Using Service Account Hash
description: Detects Silver Ticket attacks — forged TGS tickets signed with service account NTLM hash. Bypasses the KDC entirely. More stealthy than Golden Ticket as no DC authentication required.
remediation: Rotate service account passwords regularly (use gMSA)
Enable PAC validation — KB5008380
Monitor for service authentications without DC-side Kerberos events

title: Diamond Ticket — Stealthy TGT Modification (Evades Golden Ticket Detection)
description: Diamond Ticket modifies a legitimate TGT in-memory rather than forging from scratch. Harder to detect than Golden Ticket as real TGT was requested from KDC. Rubeus diamond command uses /tgtdeleg or krbtgt AES key.
remediation: Monitor Rubeus.exe execution (process creation events)
Implement PAC validation KB5008380 (checks SID consistency)
Alert on /tgtdeleg usage — Kerberos delegation from unexpected accounts

title: Unconstrained Delegation — TGT Capture via Coercion
description: Computers with Unconstrained Delegation store TGTs for all authenticating users in LSASS. Attacker coerces DC authentication to this host, captures DC's TGT, and uses it to compromise the domain.
remediation: Remove Unconstrained Delegation from non-DC computers
Enable "Account is sensitive and cannot be delegated" on privileged accounts
Use Constrained Delegation or Resource-Based Constrained Delegation instead

title: Constrained Delegation S4U2Proxy Abuse
description: Abuses S4U2Self/S4U2Proxy extensions to impersonate any user to services the compromised account is allowed to delegate to. Requires control of an account with msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo attribute set.
remediation: Audit msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo attributes
Enable "Account is sensitive and cannot be delegated" for privileged users
Prefer RBCD over Constrained Delegation where possible

title: PSExec-Style Lateral Movement — Remote Service Creation via SMB
description: Detects PSExec-style execution via SMB IPC$ share and Windows Service Manager. Creates a random-named service, executes payload, then removes. impacket-psexec, sysinternals psexec, and Cobalt Strike use this pattern.
remediation: Restrict admin shares (ADMIN$, C$) access
Block lateral movement via host-based firewall rules
Monitor Event ID 7045 for short-lived random services

title: WMI Remote Command Execution — Lateral Movement
description: Detects WMI-based remote command execution (impacket-wmiexec, CrackMapExec WMI). WMI semi-interactive shell spawns cmd.exe via WmiPrvSE.exe. Harder to detect than PSExec — no service creation.
remediation: Disable WMI remote access via Windows Firewall
Monitor WmiPrvSE.exe child processes
Restrict DCOM permissions (Component Services)

title: Evil-WinRM — Windows Remote Management Shell Access
description: Detects Evil-WinRM connections for remote PowerShell shell access. Uses WinRM (TCP 5985/5986). Supports Pass-the-Hash and certificate auth.
remediation: Restrict WinRM access via firewall (only from management hosts)
Enable WinRM HTTPS only (port 5986)
Monitor Event ID 4624 LogonType=3 from unexpected sources

title: DCOM Remote Execution — MMC20 Application Object Abuse
description: Detects DCOM-based lateral movement via MMC20.Application, ShellWindows, ShellBrowserWindow, or ExcelDDE. Leaves minimal artifacts — no service creation or WMI events.
remediation: Restrict DCOM permissions via DCOMCNFG
Block outbound RPC (135, 49152-65535) between workstations

title: Pass-the-Hash — NTLM Authentication with Stolen Hash
description: Detects Pass-the-Hash attacks where NTLM hash is used instead of password for authentication. Pattern: NewCredentials logon (type 9) followed by network authentication, or CrackMapExec -H flag usage.
remediation: Enforce Protected Users group for privileged accounts (blocks NTLM)
Disable NTLM authentication where possible (use Kerberos)
Enable Network access: Restrict NTLM (Group Policy)

title: WinRS Lateral Movement — Low-Footprint Remote Shell
description: WinRS (winrshost.exe) provides remote shell via WinRM without creating PowerShell process. Lower detection profile than Evil-WinRM. Used in CRTP curriculum for stealthy lateral movement.
remediation: Monitor winrshost.exe process creation events
Restrict WinRM access to management jump servers only
Audit Event ID 91 (WinRM session start)

title: AtExec — Scheduled Task Remote Execution
description: impacket-atexec creates a remote scheduled task, executes command, retrieves output, then deletes the task. Leaves Event ID 4698 (task creation) and 4699 (task deletion) within seconds.
remediation: Monitor Event IDs 4698/4699 pairs with short intervals
Audit scheduled task creation by non-administrative accounts
Restrict Task Scheduler remote access

title: RDP Access with Pass-the-Hash via xfreerdp
description: Detects RDP Pass-the-Hash using xfreerdp /pth flag or enabling RDP via CrackMapExec rdp module followed by restricted admin mode connection.
remediation: Enable Network Level Authentication (NLA)
Disable Restricted Admin Mode
Monitor RDP connections via NTLM authentication

title: Password Spraying — Distributed Authentication Failures
description: Password spraying uses single password against many accounts to avoid lockout. Detection pattern: many 4625 failures from same source IP, each for different username, within short timeframe.
remediation: Implement account lockout policy (lockout after 5-10 failures, 30 min lockout)
Deploy fine-grained password policy for privileged accounts
Monitor for low-and-slow spray patterns

title: Kerbrute Username Enumeration via Kerberos Pre-Auth
description: Kerbrute enumerates valid usernames using Kerberos AS-REQ pre-auth errors. Valid users return different error code than invalid users, allowing enumeration without authentication. Doesn't trigger lockouts.
remediation: Rate limit Kerberos AS-REQ from single source
Monitor bulk 4768 errors (ResultCode=0x6) from same IP
Use honeypot accounts that alert on any auth attempt

title: NTLM Relay via Responder — SMB Signing Bypass
description: NTLM relay captures authentication from poisoned LLMNR/NBT-NS and relays it to target systems without SMB signing. Enables code execution or AD object modification without knowing the password.
remediation: Enable SMB Signing (required) on all systems
Disable NTLM authentication (use Kerberos)
Disable LLMNR and NBT-NS (prevents poisoning)

title: Credential Stuffing — Breached Credential List Against AD
description: Testing breached username:password pairs against Active Directory. Pattern similar to password spraying but with matched pairs — each account tested with its previously-breached password.
remediation: Compare AD user passwords against HaveIBeenPwned database
Enable Microsoft Entra Password Protection (leaked password detection)
Implement MFA as defense against credential stuffing

title: Default Credential Testing — Common Service Account Passwords
description: Testing default credentials for common service accounts (admin/admin, administrator/password123, sa/sa, etc.) against SMB and other services.
remediation: Disable default accounts (Guest, local Administrator)
Rename default administrator account
Enforce complex passwords during account creation

title: AdminSDHolder ACL Modification — Persistent Privileged Access
description: AdminSDHolder is a template object whose DACL is propagated to all protected groups every 60 minutes by SDPropagator. Adding a backdoor ACE to AdminSDHolder grants persistent access that survives direct group membership removal.
remediation: Monitor Event 5136 on AdminSDHolder object
Review AdminSDHolder DACL quarterly
Alert on manual SDPropagator execution (Invoke-SDPropagator command)

title: DSRM Account Backdoor — Remote DC Login via Directory Services Restore Mode
description: DSRM (Directory Services Restore Mode) account is a local administrator on every DC. By setting DSRMAdminLogonBehavior=2, DSRM account can be used for remote network authentication — persistent DC backdoor.
remediation: Remove or reset DSRMAdminLogonBehavior to 0
Regularly rotate DSRM password (at least annually)
Monitor registry key DSRMAdminLogonBehavior for value changes

title: Skeleton Key — Mimikatz LSASS Patch for Master Password
description: Skeleton Key patches LSASS on the DC to accept any password ("skeleton key") for any domain account in addition to the real password. Non-persistent — cleared on DC reboot. Requires DA privileges.
remediation: Deploy Credential Guard (prevents LSASS patching)
Monitor Event ID 4611 (Trusted Logon Process) on DCs
Alert on LSASS memory writes from non-system processes on DCs

title: Custom SSP Installation — mimilib Credential Logger
description: Installing mimilib.dll as Security Support Provider (SSP) causes Windows to log all authentication credentials to C:\Windows\System32\kiwissp.log. Survives reboots as DLL registered in LSA Security Packages registry key.
commands: mimikatz misc::memssp  # In-memory SSP (no disk write)
all_commands: mimikatz misc::memssp  # In-memory SSP (no disk write)
remediation: Monitor registry key "Security Packages" for unauthorized DLL additions
Monitor for kiwissp.log creation in System32
Use Windows Defender ASR rule "Block credential stealing from LSASS

title: SID History Injection — Hidden Domain Admin Privileges
description: SID History stores SIDs from previous domain migrations. Injecting DA SID into regular user's SID History grants DA-level access without group membership — bypasses membership-based monitoring.
remediation: Enable SID filtering on all domain trusts
Monitor Event 4765/4766 for unexpected SID History additions
Audit users with sIDHistory set to privileged SIDs

title: WMI Event Subscription — Fileless Persistence
description: WMI event subscriptions (EventFilter + EventConsumer + FilterToConsumerBinding) provide fileless persistence triggered by system events. CommandLineEventConsumer executes arbitrary commands. Very stealthy — no scheduled task or registry Run key.
remediation: Enable Sysmon WMI subscription logging (Event IDs 19, 20, 21)
Monitor WmiPrvSE.exe child processes
Audit existing WMI subscriptions: Get-WmiObject -Namespace root/subscription -Class __EventFilter

title: Registry Run Key — Local Persistence
description: Adds executable to HKCU or HKLM Run registry keys for execution at logon/startup. Classic local persistence mechanism.
remediation: Monitor registry Run key modifications (Event 4657)
Enable Autoruns monitoring and alerting
Restrict HKLM Run key writes to SYSTEM/Administrators

title: Custom Network Provider — NPPSPY Credential Capture on Logon
description: NPPSPY registers a malicious DLL as a Network Provider that receives all plaintext credentials during Windows logon. Survives reboots. Credentials written to C:\NPPSpy.txt or similar.
remediation: Monitor NetworkProvider registry key modifications
Audit registered Network Providers on all systems
Implement allowlisting for approved Network Provider DLLs

title: Persistent Constrained Delegation — msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo Modification
description: Attacker with GenericWrite/WriteDACL sets msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo on a controlled account to allow impersonation to sensitive services. Provides persistent impersonation path that survives password resets.
remediation: Alert on any msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo modification (Event 5136)
Audit delegation attributes quarterly
Enable "Account is sensitive and cannot be delegated" for DA accounts

title: RBCD Full Chain — MachineAccountQuota to Computer Account Compromise
description: Full RBCD attack: (1) Create fake computer via MAQ, (2) Write fake computer SID to target's msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity, (3) Use S4U2Self to impersonate DA to target service, (4) Use S4U2Proxy to get service ticket. Results in full control of target computer as Administrator.
remediation: Set MachineAccountQuota to 0 (Critical — prevents all MAQ-based RBCD)
Alert on msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity modifications (Event 5136)
Monitor for sequence: 4741 → 5136 within short timeframe

title: RBCD via NTLM Relay — --delegate-access Flag
description: impacket-ntlmrelayx --delegate-access automatically performs RBCD attack during relay: creates computer account and sets RBCD on victim computer in single operation. Most automated RBCD attack path.
remediation: Enable LDAP signing (prevents relay to LDAP)
Enable SMB signing on all hosts
Set MachineAccountQuota to 0

title: Bronze Bit — CVE-2020-17049 Constrained Delegation Bypass
description: CVE-2020-17049 (Bronze Bit) bypasses the constrained delegation restriction on forwarding non-forwardable service tickets. Allows S4U2Proxy with tickets that should not be forwardable. Patched in November 2020.
remediation: Apply November 2020 Kerberos security updates (KB4586786)
Monitor S4U2Proxy requests with non-forwardable tickets

title: S4U2Self — Service Account Impersonation via Protocol Transition
description: S4U2Self (Protocol Transition) allows a service account with trusted for delegation to request a service ticket to itself on behalf of any user. Used as first step before S4U2Proxy or standalone for token impersonation.
remediation: Enable "Account is sensitive and cannot be delegated" for DA accounts
Monitor S4U2Self requests for privileged accounts

title: RBCD Cleanup — Attacker Removes Evidence (msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity)
description: After RBCD attack, attacker cleans up by removing msDS-AllowedToActOnBehalfOfOtherIdentity and deleting fake computer account. Removal of these indicators is itself suspicious.
remediation: Alert on BOTH setting AND removal of delegation attributes
Retain AD change history in SIEM for 90+ days

title: Powermad — New-MachineAccount Computer Creation via LDAP
description: Powermad.ps1 New-MachineAccount creates computer accounts via direct LDAP without needing local admin or Active Directory Users and Computers. Exploits MachineAccountQuota default of 10.
remediation: Set MachineAccountQuota = 0 (elimnates Powermad attack path)
Monitor for New-MachineAccount PowerShell execution
sast_code_analysis:

title: Cross-Forest Kerberoasting — Foreign Domain SPN Enumeration
description: Kerberoasting targeting service accounts in foreign trusted domains. Requires authentication in source domain, requests TGS for foreign domain SPNs using inter-realm ticket referral chain.
remediation: Enable SID Filtering on all forest trusts
Restrict service accounts in trusted domains to strong passwords/gMSA
Monitor cross-realm TGS requests with RC4 encryption

title: ExtraSID Attack — Inter-Realm Golden Ticket with Parent Domain SID
description: ExtraSID injects parent/target domain's Enterprise Admin SID (S-1-5-21-*-519) into the inter-realm TGT. When used against parent domain, grants EA-level access without legitimate credentials in that domain.
remediation: Enable SID Filtering on ALL trust relationships (breaks SID History migration — plan accordingly)
Monitor inter-realm ticket requests with anomalous PAC content
Deploy Microsoft Defender for Identity golden/inter-realm ticket detection

title: Trust Key Extraction — lsadump::trust for Cross-Forest Silver Ticket
description: Trust keys (Inter-Realm trust account hash) are stored in LSASS on DCs. Extraction via lsadump::trust or secretsdump enables forging of inter-realm tickets for cross-forest access.
remediation: Reset trust account passwords (netdom resetpwd)
Monitor trust account hash access on DCs
Enable auditing on trust-related LDAP attributes

title: PAM Trust Abuse — Shadow Principals in Privileged Access Management
description: PAM (Privileged Access Management) trust allows bastion forest security groups to have time-limited shadow memberships in production forest groups. Abuse: attacker compromises bastion forest and abuses shadow principal mappings.
remediation: Treat bastion forest with same security as production forest
Review shadow principal mappings regularly
Monitor cross-forest auth events from bastion accounts

title: Domain Trust Enumeration via LDAP
description: Detects LDAP enumeration of domain trust relationships via trustedDomain object class queries. Provides attack map of connected forests.
remediation: Monitor bulk LDAP reads on trustedDomain objects
Implement AD canary objects in trust enumeration paths

title: Foreign Security Principal — Cross-Domain Group Membership Enumeration
description: Foreign Security Principals (FSPs) represent accounts from trusted domains that are members of local groups. Enumeration identifies potential escalation paths via group membership in target domain.
remediation: Audit foreign security principals in sensitive groups
Remove unnecessary cross-domain group memberships

title: Child-to-Parent Domain Escalation via SID History Injection
description: Compromising child domain krbtgt allows forging inter-realm TGT with parent domain Enterprise Admin SID injected via SID history mechanism. Effectively grants EA in parent domain from child domain compromise.
remediation: Enable SID Filtering between parent and child domains
Alert when -519 (EA) SID appears in tickets from child domain users
Treat child domain compromise as parent domain compromise
